jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
**About The MORE Real Estate Group**


At The MORE Real Estate Group, WHO we are is far more important than WHAT we do.


We are a high\-performing, relationship\-driven real estate team built on trust, results, relentless client advocacy, and a commitment to creating experiences people can feel. We work hard, play hard, love hard, and believe growth happens when people are willing to get uncomfortable in pursuit of becoming better.


We are looking for a visionary, execution\-oriented **Director of Operations** who is equally passionate about people, systems, innovation, and culture. This is not a traditional operations role. We are looking for someone who can help architect the future of our business through operational excellence, AI integration, scalable systems, and leadership support, while fiercely protecting the heart of our culture.


This person leads with ownership. They see opportunities where others see obstacles. They thrive in a fast\-paced environment, embrace change, and bring solutions instead of problems. They are highly relational and love helping great people perform at an even higher level.

**Position Overview**


The Director of Operations serves as the operational leader of The MORE Real Estate Group and is responsible for helping scale the business through exceptional leadership, operational excellence, systems development, AI integration, and strategic execution.


This is not a traditional operations role.


We are looking for a visionary executor, someone who can think strategically while implementing tactically. Someone who can build systems, develop people, drive accountability, leverage technology, and help ownership translate ideas into results.


The Director of Operations will partner directly with ownership to:

* Improve operational efficiency
* Build scalable systems and workflows
* Integrate AI and automation throughout the organization
* Increase profitability and productivity
* Enhance agent and client experiences
* Protect and strengthen company culture
* Drive accountability and execution
* Support long\-term growth and scalability


This person must love people as much as processes and understand that great systems should enhance the human experience, not replace it.

**Compensation**

* **Salary: $100,000\-$120,000 base; On\-target earnings (OTE): $125,000\-$150,000**

  

**Compensation:**
$100,000 \- $120,000

 **Responsibilities:****Leadership \& Team Development**

* Lead, coach, and develop the Client Experience Team and support staff.
* Hire, onboard, and train new team members.
* Conduct regular one\-on\-one meetings and performance reviews.
* Create clear expectations, accountability standards, and growth plans.
* Foster a culture of ownership, excellence, collaboration, and contribution.
* Support team members in both professional and personal growth.
* Identify staffing needs and help build organizational capacity as the company grows.

**Operations \& Systems Leadership**

* Oversee daily operations across the organization.
* Develop, implement, and continuously improve systems and processes.
* Build scalable workflows that improve efficiency, consistency, and profitability.
* Create, document, and maintain SOPs, playbooks, and operational manuals.
* Ensure operational consistency across all departments.
* Lead implementation of strategic initiatives and company projects.
* Manage project timelines and ensure accountability for execution.
* Coordinate communication between ownership, agents, and staff.

**AI \& Innovation Leadership**

* Lead the integration of AI tools and automation across the organization.
* Identify opportunities where AI can improve productivity, service, communication, and operational efficiency.
* Build AI\-assisted workflows for:
* Recruiting
* Marketing
* Client Experience
* Operations
* Training
* Reporting
* Communication
* Train team members on AI best practices and implementation.
* Stay ahead of emerging technology and industry trends.
* Continuously identify opportunities to future\-proof the business through innovation.

**Business Performance \& Financial Oversight**

* Manage budgets, invoices, vendor relationships, and financial reporting.
* Monitor profitability and identify opportunities to improve margins.
* Track key operational and financial KPIs.
* Assist ownership with forecasting, business planning, and goal setting.
* Maintain accountability to company budgets and performance targets.
* Build dashboards and reporting systems that provide visibility into business performance.

**Agent Support \& Accountability**

* Track agent production, pipeline activity, conversion rates, and business performance.
* Support leadership coaching initiatives through data\-driven reporting.
* Implement systems that improve lead conversion and client experience.
* Help drive adoption of company systems and standards.

**Client Experience Excellence**

* Elevate and maintain a luxury\-level client experience.
* Ensure operational excellence throughout every stage of the client journey.
* Partner with transaction and client care teams to improve communication and consistency.
* Identify opportunities to create memorable client experiences at scale.
* Protect the reputation and service standards of The MORE Real Estate Group.

**Events, Partnerships \& Community Engagement**

* Oversee planning and execution of client events, appreciation events, workshops, conferences, retreats, and team meetings.
* Manage event budgets, logistics, vendors, and timelines.
* Coordinate travel arrangements and company experiences.
* Develop relationships with vendors, sponsors, strategic partners, and community organizations.
* Secure sponsorships and partnership opportunities.
* Serve as a professional ambassador for The MORE Real Estate Group.

  

**Qualifications:****Who You Are**


You are:

* A servant leader who develops people.
* Highly relational and emotionally intelligent.
* Exceptionally organized and operationally m

jobs.title: Program Director - Human Resources
jobs.description:
**About Redwood Community Services**
------------------------------------


Redwood Community Services (RCS) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to strengthening individuals, families, and communities through behavioral health, child welfare, youth development, housing, and support services. Our work changes lives every day, and our employees make that possible.


We are seeking a highly qualified, strategic, and experienced **Director of Human Resources** who brings strong leadership, sound judgment, and a passion for developing people and strengthening organizational culture. The ideal candidate is a collaborative HR professional who thrives in a dynamic environment, excels in driving employee engagement, ensuring compliance, and aligning human resources strategies with organizational goals. If you are a forward\-thinking leader with a proven ability to build strong teams, enhance HR operations, and serve as a trusted partner to leadership, we would love to meet you.

**What You'll Do**
------------------


Key responsibilities include:

* Provide strategic direction and leadership for all HR operations, including recruitment, onboarding, employee relations, compliance, compensation, benefits, and workforce planning.
* Serve as a member of the Leadership Team, providing guidance and recommendations related to organizational development, succession planning, risk management, and employee engagement.
* Develop, implement, and continuously improve HR policies, procedures, systems, and processes to ensure efficient, compliant, and customer\-focused services.
* Oversee agency\-wide recruitment, hiring, retention, onboarding, and employee development strategies to attract and retain a qualified workforce.
* Provide consultation and support to managers and supervisors regarding employee relations, performance management, conflict resolution, investigations, and workplace concerns.
* Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local employment laws, licensing requirements, accreditation standards, and agency policies.
* Oversee employment compliance areas including leave administration, workers’ compensation, workplace safety, audits, and regulatory requirements.
* Lead compensation and benefits strategies, including classification reviews, market analysis, benefits administration, and recommendations for organizational needs.
* Develop and analyze HR metrics, reports, and dashboards to identify trends and support data\-driven decision\-making.
* Identify opportunities to improve HR operations through technology, innovation, and continuous quality improvement initiatives.
* Supervise, mentor, and develop Human Resources staff while fostering accountability, collaboration, and professional growth.
* Represent RCS in community partnerships, workforce development activities, professional organizations, and employment\-related matters.
* Participate in agency\-wide initiatives, events, trainings, and special projects as assigned by the Chief Executive Officer.

**What We're Looking For:**
---------------------------

* Associate degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field required; Bachelor’s degree preferred.
* HR certification preferred.
* 5\+ years of progressive Human Resources leadership experience.
* Strong knowledge of California labor laws, HR practices, and compliance requirements.
* Proven ability to lead teams, manage complex HR functions, and support organizational goals.
* Excellent communication, problem\-solving, and interpersonal skills.

**Additional Requirements**
---------------------------

* Valid California Driver's License with a clean driving record
* Ability to successfully complete DOJ/FBI fingerprint clearance, CACI clearance, TB screening, physical, and other pre\-employment requirements
* CPR/First Aid certification (or ability to obtain if required)

**Why Join RCS?**
-----------------


When you join Redwood Community Services, you're joining a mission\-driven organization where your work directly supports the people who provide life\-changing services throughout our communities.


We offer:

* Meaningful work with a respected nonprofit organization
* Collaborative and supportive team environment
* Opportunities for professional development
* Stable, mission\-focused employment
* Comprehensive benefits package (for eligible employees)
* The opportunity to make a real impact every day

**Apply Today**
---------------


If you're passionate about accuracy, enjoy working behind the scenes to support employees, and want to be part of an organization that changes lives, we'd love to hear from you.

jobs.title: Deputy Director, Lending and Partnerships
jobs.description:
The International Rescue Committee (IRC) responds to the world's worst humanitarian crises, helping to restore health, safety, education, economic wellbeing, and power to people devastated by conflict and disaster. Founded in 1933 at the call of Albert Einstein, the IRC is one of the world's largest international humanitarian non\-governmental organizations (INGO), at work in more than 40 countries and 29 U.S. cities helping people to survive, reclaim control of their future and strengthen their communities. A force for humanity, IRC employees deliver lasting impact by restoring safety, dignity and hope to millions. If you're a solutions\-driven, passionate change\-maker, come join us in positively impacting the lives of millions of people world\-wide for a better future.

**Background**


The IRC's Center for Economic Opportunity (CEO) is a certified community development financial institution (CDFI) and 501(c)(3\) organization incorporated in 2011, and a non\-controlled subsidiary of the International Rescue Committee (IRC). CEO's primary mission is to unlock access to credit to help create a fair financial system where low\-income people can realize their potential to build better lives and prosper communities. CEO specializes in making small\-dollar consumer and commercial loans to refugees, immigrants, and other low\-income individuals through a robust national network of lending partners operating across 17\+ states. We seek a dynamic leader to help drive our lending operations and deepen our community partnerships.

**Job Overview**


The Deputy Director, Lending and Partnerships oversees daily operations of CEO’s underwriting and lending activity, lending and borrower data analysis and technical support to CEO’s lending and service delivery partners. Reporting to the Director of Credit Strategy, this individual co\-leads the lending team, manages key institutional and community relationships, and helps drive loan production while ensuring quality underwriting standards and mission alignment. This role is ideal for a strategic, mission\-driven professional who is equally comfortable building partnerships with service organizations, ensuring data\-driven operations and managing fast\-paced, borrower\-centered lending in low\-income communities.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Lending Operations**

* Manage day\-to\-day lending operations, including pipeline management, underwriting oversight, and internal and external loan committee preparation.
* Supervise and mentor a team of loan officers and origination specialists providing guidance on loan product fit, underwriting, partner support and borrower engagement.
* Review and approve loan applications, term sheets, and loan recommendations prior to credit committee presentation.
* Ensure loan production and deployment goals are met while maintaining strong credit quality and mission alignment.
* Manage the loan pipeline tracking system; provide regular reporting on volume, product mix, and projected closings to senior leadership.
* Coordinate closely with the servicing team to ensure smooth transitions from origination through closing and into portfolio management.

 **Credit Quality \& Underwriting Standards**

* Maintain and support updating pf lending policies, underwriting guidelines, and credit standards in coordination with senior leadership.
* Ensure consistency and rigor in underwriting across all loan products and borrower types.
* Identify opportunities to refine product structures or develop new products in response to market needs and community demand.
* Support the credit committee process, including preparing and presenting materials and facilitating decision\-making discussions.

 **Partnership Development \& External Relations**

* Cultivate and manage strategic relationships community\-based organizations that generate referrals, lending as a service agreements and co\-lending opportunities.
* Oversee delivery of high\-quality training and technical support to CEO’s lending partners, ensuring rapid and effective uptake of CEO’s business systems and procedures.
* Co\-develop, in close coordination with leadership and external relations, a partnership strategy that expands the organization's referral network, increases loan volume, and deepens community trust.
* Manage formal partnership agreements, Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs), and co\-lending or participation loan arrangements with partners.
* Collaborate with the external relations team to support grant applications, CDFI Fund awards, and funder relationships that require lending data, program descriptions, or partnership documentation.

**Business Development \& Market Strategy**

* Identify underserved market segments, geographic gaps, or product needs, and recommend strategies to expand lending reach.
* Lead outreach campaigns and marketing efforts in coordination with communications staff to promote loan products and raise awareness among target borrowers.
* Support the organization's annual lending goals and strategic plan by supporting the development and implementation of business development work plans.

**Team Leadership \& Organizational Development**

* Supervise, coach, and evaluate a team of lending and partnership staff; foster a collaborative, accountable, and mission\-driven team culture.
* Assist in the recruitment, hiring, and onboarding of new lending team members.
* Lead or participate in cross\-departmental initiatives related to systems improvement, product development, impact measurement, and organizational learning.

 **Compliance \& Reporting**

* Ensure lending activities comply with applicable regulatory requirements, investor covenants, and CDFI Fund program requirements.
* Contribute to CDFI Fund reporting (AMIS, Transaction Level Reporting) and other external compliance reporting as needed.
* Support impact measurement by ensuring accurate tracking of borrower demographics, loan purpose, job creation, and other mission\-related metri

jobs.company: Jewish Community Center of the East Bay
jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
jobs.description:
**Jewish Community Center East Bay**
**Chief Development Officer (CDO)**
========================================================================


JCC East Bay seeks a fundraising executive to complete the Capital and Endowment campaign to fund the in\-process East Bay Jewish Community Campus buildout and drive Annual Fund solicitations to strengthen both programming and Campus infrastructure.


The JCC East Bay will mark a historic milestone in its 47\-year history by opening its three\-acre East Bay Jewish Community Campus and uniting many Jewish organizations in one central location. The Campus offers an innovative model for collaboration and engagement across intellectual, cultural, spiritual and social life, with opening celebrations scheduled for early 2027\.


This Campus is the first of its kind in the East Bay and is located in Oakland’s Rockridge neighborhood. Anchored by expanded JCC East Bay facilities, the site will be home to a network of up to 20 Jewish nonprofit organizations and will bring together early childhood education, arts and culture, wellness, learning and community in one vibrant location rooted in Jewish values. Encompassing over 128,000 square feet, the site was previously occupied by Dreyer's Grand Ice Cream headquarters and Yoshi’s Restaurant \& Jazz Club.


The Campus offers outdoor spaces that encourage community connection and a platform for programming in the central courtyard that includes a meadow area, reflection garden, ball court and seating areas. The Campus will also feature a performance venue with capacity to entertain over 200 patrons, flexible multi\-use spaces and an outdoor deck. These features provide the East Bay with a sought\-after setting for year\-round programming. Events will center on arts, ideas and Jewish culture, featuring local, national and international artists and thinkers.


Enclosed areas include:


* Office space, including shared meetings rooms and other facilities in the main building
* A spacious early childhood education center with outdoor play areas and a sensory room
* A health and wellness studio
* Classrooms for adult learning
* A kosher café
* A catering and teaching kitchen
* An intimate library
* A dedicated teen center

The JCC East Bay is approximately halfway through its $125M Capital Campaign. The property was purchased by a real estate developer and philanthropist who began redeveloping it in 2019\. The property is within close walking distance to the Rockridge BART station, and the neighborhood is home to a broad range of small businesses, restaurants, cafes and shops, with a strong residential presence.


The Campus operates on a shared\-expense model that is designed to fully and sustainably fund the facility as a constantly refreshed host to the nonprofits it supports. The financial model relies on rental income from retail business, a shared expense model with onsite partners, and a Campus endowment to offset common operating expenses such as security, utilities, maintenance, landscaping, infrastructure and periodic upgrades.


The CDO will orchestrate all JCC and Campus donor solicitations and stewardship, enabling the JCC to play a central role in advancing the Jewish community in the East Bay.


**BASIC FUNCTION**

Reporting to and working in concert with JCC East Bay’s Chief Executive Officer and serving as a key member of the Executive Team, the CDO will complete the Capital Campaign and will also optimize earned revenue from ticketed events, contributed revenue from individual and foundation donors, business and other sponsorships, and in\-kind contributions that are routed to Capital, Endowment and Annual fundraising.


The CDO orchestrates all JCC donor engagement, solicitation and stewardship, enabling the JCC to play a central role in advancing the Jewish community in the East Bay. Additionally, the CDO guides donor engagement and stewardship and harvests information on Campus users and prospective users in order to help bring this transformational project to completion.


The CDO manages a current fundraising team of three employees. As an operating unit this team is responsible for:


* **Leveraging and Analyzing Data** on donors, prospective donors and all individuals and organizations that use the Campus space. This information will be used to target, qualify, approach and build a case for support from donors of significant capacity.
* **Major Gift Solicitations**, including developing a pipeline of major gifts for the Annual Fund and Principal and Transformational gifts for Capital and Endowment Campaigns, with a robust program of direct solicitations by members of the team, the CDO, the CEO and Board members.
* **Planned Giving Solicitations \& Grants**, including developing case statements and grant materials, targeting family and other foundations, soliciting government grants, and developing business sponsorships for programs offered at JCC East Bay.
* **Special Events (Ribbon Cutting and Campus Opening Events)**, ensuring the Board is engaged in fundraising, working closely with honorees to identify those in their network that could provide philanthropic support and strategically developing corporate sponsorships.

The CDO will work with the CEO (and Board’s Development and Capital Campaign Committees) to: set long\-term and annual fundraising targets and strategies; hire staff support for fundraising; actively cultivate and directly solicit leadership\-level donors; involve the CEO and Board members as appropriate; and be responsible for other fundraising\-related tasks typically executed by a CDO of a similarly scaled organization. Responsibilities include:


SHAPE STRATEGY \& STANDARDS


* Lead development of meaningful annual and campaign fundraising plans.
* Set fundraising objectives and track progress at the individual and team levels, including for the CEO and Board.
* Shape the case for support, budgets, proposals, cultivation events and reporting.
* Implement strong sta

jobs.title: Master's Program Director (5157C) - Department of Statistics
jobs.description:
Master's Program Director (5157C) \- Department of Statistics About Berkeley
  
  

At the University of California, Berkeley, we are dedicated to fostering a community where everyone feels welcome and can thrive. Our culture of openness, freedom and belonging make it a special place for students, faculty and staff.
  
  

As a world\-leading institution, Berkeley is known for its academic and research excellence, public mission, diverse student body, and commitment to equity and social justice. Since our founding in 1868, we have driven innovation, creating global intellectual, economic and social value.
  
  

We are looking for applicants who reflect California's diversity and want to be part of an inclusive, equity\-focused community that views education as a matter of social justice. Please consider whether your values align with our Guiding Values and Principles , Principles of Community , and Strategic Plan .
  
  

At UC Berkeley, we believe that learning is a fundamental part of working, and provide space for supportive colleague communities via numerous employee resource groups (staff organizations). Our goal is for everyone on the Berkeley campus to feel supported and equipped to realize their full potential. We actively support this by providing all of our full\-time staff employees with at least 80 hours (10 days) of paid time per year to engage in professional development activities. Find out more about how you can grow your career at UC Berkeley.
  
Departmental Overview
  
  

The Statistics Department, in the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society (CDSS), was originally established in 1938 as the first center in the country for statistical research. Since its inception, graduates and faculty of Berkeley have shaped the foundations of statistics. The department continues to be recognized as one of the preeminent centers for Probability and Statistics in the world.
  
  

The department places a strong emphasis on research through partnerships working with institutions and industry partners that include: Lawrence Berkeley National Lab (LBNL), the Helen Wills Neuroscience Institute (HWNI), the Sloan Institute, the Simons Institute, the Berkeley Institute for Data Science (BIDS), and private\-sector industry leaders in tech, finance, and healthcare. We collaborate closely with departments and centers such as: Astronomy, Bioengineering, Biostatistics, Berkeley AI Research (BAIR), the Center for Computational Biology (CCB), Computational Precision Health (CPH), Demography, Economics, Electrical Engineering \& Computer Sciences (EECS), the Goldman School of Public Policy (GSPP), Integrative Biology (IB), Mathematics, Molecular \& Cell Biology (MCB), Neuroscience, Berkeley AI Research (BAIR), Computational Precision Health (CPH), Physics, and Sociology.
  
  

The Statistics Department has roughly 50 active faculty and emeritus faculty, 5 lecturers, 15 researchers, 150 graduate students, 500 undergraduate students, and 14 career staff positions. The faculty have a dizzying array of honors and awards, including the National Medal of Science, the COPSS award (the Statistics "Nobel"), and seven members of the National Academy of Sciences. The department is highly attractive to Visiting Faculty, Visiting Scholars, Postdoctoral Scholars, and students from around the world and currently enrolls 7,000 students in its courses annually.
  
  

The Master's in Statistics and Data Science is a full\-time professional degree program completed over two to three semesters and designed to prepare students for careers in industry. The program enrolls approximately 100 students annually.
  
  

The Master's Program Director provides strategic leadership and operational oversight for the Master's in Statistics and Data Science program and plays a central role in advancing the program's academic, administrative, and student success objectives. Working closely with the Department Chair, Director of Administration, Director of Student Services, he Master's Program Faculty Committee and faculty leadership, the Director contributes to long\-range planning, policy development, program operations, student engagement, and continuous program improvement.
  
  

Under the general supervision of the Director of Student Services, the Director independently manages the administration and daily operations of the program, applying advanced expertise in graduate student services, academic advising, admissions and enrollment management, financial aid administration, career development, program planning, and student engagement. The Director develops and implements initiatives that enhance the student experience, strengthen recruitment and yield outcomes, support student success, and promote operational excellence.
  
  

The Director oversees complex administrative and operational functions essential to the continued growth and success of the program and serves as a key liaison among students, faculty, staff, alumni, employers, and external partners.
  
Application Review Date
  
  

**The First Review Date for this job is:** July 22, 2026 \- Open Until Filled
  
Responsibilities
  
  

**Program Oversight:** With a high degree of autonomy, oversees the operational, administrative, and strategic functions of the Master's Program. Develops and implements initiatives to support program growth, enhance the student experience, improve operational effectiveness, and ensure alignment with departmental and institutional goals.
  
  

Leads program operations across multiple stakeholders, including faculty, lecturers, staff, student employees, and external partners, to support the successful implementation of academic and co\-curricular initiatives.
  
Working closely with the Master's Chair, oversees planning, timelines, communications, and execution of program activities and initiatives, ensuring efficient operations and a high\-quality student experience.
  
Develops strategic goals, assesses program performan

jobs.title: Director of Operations and Community Events, 92NY Nursery School
jobs.description:
Job Summary:

The Director of Operations and Community Events is a key member of the Nursery School leadership team and is responsible for the successful management of school operations, administrative infrastructure, family experience, and community events.

  

The Director ensures the smooth day\-to\-day functioning of the school, creating a welcoming, organized, and engaging environment for students, families, faculty, and staff. This role oversees front\-office operations, government compliance, facilities management, technology operations, scheduling and the annual school calendar to ensure seamless day\-to\-day execution. The Director leads the planning and execution of Nursery school events and community\-building activities that strengthen family engagement, celebrate school traditions, and foster a vibrant Nursery School culture.

  

The ideal candidate is a highly visible, collaborative leader with exceptional, operational, organizational and interpersonal skills.


Job Responsibilities Include:
*School Operations and Administration*

* Serve as the primary point person for operational matters within the Nursery School and ensure the smooth functioning of daily school activities.
* Provide leadership, direction, and supervision to the Operations Coordinator ensuring exceptional service to students, families, faculty, and visitors.
* Serve as an operational partner in the execution of the Nursery School Admissions process.
* Partner closely with faculty and staff to identify, address, and resolve operational concerns and facility\-related issues.
* Develop, implement, and maintain operational procedures and systems that improve efficiency and service delivery.
* Manage school calendars, schedules, logistics, and administrative processes.
* Produce operational reports and management dashboards to support decision\-making and planning.
* Lead continuous process improvement initiatives to streamline workflows and enhance the family and staff experience.
* Manage special projects and operational initiatives as assigned by the Executive Director.

*Family Experience and Community Relations*

* Serve as a visible, welcoming, and responsive presence for families and prospective families.
* Partner with school leadership to create an exceptional family experience throughout the enrollment and school lifecycle.
* Address parent inquiries and concerns with professionalism, empathy, and discretion.
* Support admissions and enrollment activities through tours, information sessions, open houses, and community events.
* Foster a culture of hospitality, responsiveness, and partnership throughout the Nursery School.

*Community Events and School Traditions*

* Develop and manage the annual calendar of Nursery School community events and special programs.
* Lead the planning and execution of school\-wide events including welcome events, holiday celebrations, parent education programs, family festivals, graduation, and other community\-building activities.
* Manage logistics, budgets, staffing, vendor relationships, communications, and event execution.
* Partner with faculty and administrative staff to ensure events support the educational mission and community goals of the school.
* Evaluate event participation and family feedback to continuously strengthen engagement and community connection.

*Facilities, Technology, and Safety*

* Serve as the primary liaison with Building Operations, Security, and other 92NY departments.
* Oversee the routine maintenance, space readiness, and facility improvements.
* Identify and resolve technology\-related issues in partnership with IT.
* Guide emergency preparedness planning and school safety initiatives.
* Ensure classrooms, offices, and shared spaces remain welcoming, safe, and fully operational.

*Compliance and Risk Management*

* Ensure compliance with all applicable federal, state, city, and licensing requirements.
* Maintain required records, certifications, and compliance documentation.
* Coordinate mandated reporting and ensure all required protocols are implemented and followed.
* Oversee audits, inspections, and regulatory reviews as needed.

*Student Programs and Special Initiatives*

* + Oversee operational aspects of field trips, special programs, and non\-classroom activities.
	+ Support the successful delivery of student and family programming throughout the academic year.
	+ Partner with faculty and administrators to ensure seamless execution of school initiatives and special events.


Experience, Education, \& Skills Desired:
* Bachelor's degree required; experience in education, nonprofit administration, event management, or a related field preferred.
* 7\+ years of progressively responsible experience in operations, administration, event management, school administration, or a related field.
* Demonstrated success managing complex projects, events, and operational systems.
* Strong organizational, budgeting, and project management skills.
* Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining strong attention to detail.
* Proficiency with Microsoft Office, Zoom, Brightwheel, and related administrative technologies.
* Commitment to the mission of the 92NY Nursery School and a passion for supporting children, families, and educators.


Work Environment \& Requirements:

Work Hours and Environment


40 hours/week

  

Application Instructions


Interested applicants should forward a resume and a cover letter with salary requirements.
Due to a high volume of applications that we receive, we are only able to contact those applicants whose experience most aligns with the position profile.

  

Compensation Range

**$**105K \- $115K

 *The actual compensation offered will be based on a number of factors including, but not limited to the qualifications of the applicant, years of relevant experience, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and if

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**About the Organization**

Our client is a national 501(c)(3\) nonprofit dedicated to disease research, education, and public awareness, approaching a milestone anniversary. The organization is guided by a devoted 15–16\-member Board, a Medical Advisory Committee, and an Education Council of physicians, attorneys, and lay volunteers who help keep the organization current on emerging clinical areas. The organization's name will be shared with candidates as the process advances.

**The Role**

Reporting directly to the President and working closely with the Board Chair and Board of Directors, the Executive Director is the organization's senior operational and strategic leader, overseeing administration, programs, financial management, fundraising, marketing, and community engagement. This is a New York\-based position with an immediate start date, including regular travel to a satellite office and to key markets in several other states.

**Key Responsibilities**

● Serve as the organization's chief operating and strategic leader, reporting directly to the President and working closely with the Board Chair and Board of Directors, who meet twice a year.

● Act as the organization's face and voice — comfortable promoting the mission, a concept, or an idea, and comfortable putting themselves forward as a spokesperson in front of donors, media, and partners.

● Manage and present to the Board with confidence, knowing how to read the room and work effectively with a devoted, engaged group of roughly 15–16 members; craft talking points and reports and oversee Board meeting minutes.

● Lead fundraising efforts across individuals, foundations, and corporations — the fastest\-growing of the three — including donor cultivation, sponsorships, and grant development, generally targeting a minimum of $15,000 per grant using established, well\-tested outreach protocols (including annual gala and named\-grant cycles).

● Oversee the annual operating budget and financial reporting to the Board; the organization carries no endowment; funds raised are deployed directly to mission activities, so budget discipline and cash\-flow awareness matter. Work alongside the organization's outside auditing firm, bookkeeper, and the gala's outside meeting\-planner consultant.

● Maintain a consistent in\-office presence — the Board is accustomed to and expects this — while also traveling roughly monthly to a satellite office and to key markets to represent the organization at events and national meetings.

● Partner with the organization's Medical Advisory Committee and Education Council (curriculum\-focused), made up of physicians, attorneys, and lay volunteers, to stay current on emerging clinical areas relevant to the mission.

● Oversee daily operations, staff supervision, and organizational systems; establish and maintain sound policies and best practices; review contracts and organizational commitments.

● Build and sustain relationships with donors, community leaders, partner organizations, and volunteers; oversee marketing, communications, PR, and channels such as the organization's podcast to build visibility.

**Qualifications**

● Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred.

● Minimum of 5–7 years of senior leadership or nonprofit management experience; a background in sales, marketing, pharmaceutical, healthcare, public relations, lobbying, or volunteer\-driven organizations is valuable — candidates do not need to come from healthcare specifically.

● Demonstrated experience working effectively with, and presenting confidently to, a Board of Directors.

● Proven fundraising and donor\-cultivation track record, ideally including experience growing corporate support and managing a grant/gala cycle.

● Strong financial management and budgeting skills, including experience overseeing an operating budget with the support of outside accounting/audit resources.

● Excellent writing skills and strong administrative capability — organized, hands\-on, and able to give a Board confidence that things are being handled.

● A proactive, prepared, businesslike approach to running the organization, paired with genuine presence and self\-assurance in front of others.

● High integrity, professionalism, and sound judgment.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

Compensation: $175K–$200K. The organization offers a comprehensive medical and dental plan and is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive workplace for all employees and applicants.

Pay: $175,000\.00 \- $200,000\.00 per year

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: Executive Director/Nonprofit Leadership, Fundraising, Sponsorship, Donors (Ridgefield ABC)
jobs.description:
Executive Director \| Nonprofit Leadership, Fundraising, Educational Equity \& Community ImpactRidgefield A Better Chance (RABC)

Ridgefield A Better Chance (RABC) is part of the national A Better Chance network, one of the nation’s leading educational equity organizations dedicated to increasing access to high\-quality college preparatory education for academically talented students of color.

Since 1987, Ridgefield ABC has empowered young women of color through educational opportunity, leadership development, academic enrichment, and community support. Scholars live in a residential setting supported by residential directors, academic advisors, host families, college counseling, and dedicated volunteers while attending the academically rigorous Ridgefield High School.

Ridgefield ABC is a respected and mission\-driven nonprofit organization with a highly engaged Board of Directors and strong community support. We are seeking a dynamic, relationship\-centered Executive Director to lead fundraising, nonprofit operations, strategic growth, community engagement, student\-centered programming, and organizational leadership.

The Executive Director reports directly to the Board President and works collaboratively with the Board of Directors, volunteers, staff, families, community stakeholders, and national ABC partners.

Position Overview

**Executive Director**

The Executive Director is the chief executive and strategic leader of Ridgefield ABC, responsible for advancing the organization's mission by strengthening fundraising, cultivating donor and community relationships, overseeing operations and financial stewardship, supporting student success, and partnering with the Board to ensure long\-term sustainability and growth.

The successful candidate is a collaborative, relationship\-driven nonprofit executive with a demonstrated track record in fundraising, organizational leadership, financial management, team development, and community engagement. They are passionate about educational opportunity, student achievement, leadership development, and creating an inclusive environment where scholars can thrive.

Key ResponsibilitiesFundraising \& Resource Development

* Lead the organization's fundraising strategy to ensure long\-term financial sustainability.
* Cultivate, solicit, steward, and retain individual donors, foundations, corporate partners, and community supporters.
* Partner with the Board and Fundraising Committee to execute annual giving campaigns, sponsorships, fundraising events, and major gift initiatives.
* Identify and pursue grant opportunities while supporting proposal development and reporting.
* Build a robust donor pipeline and strengthen long\-term philanthropic relationships.
* Develop innovative revenue strategies that expand organizational capacity and mission impact.

Strategic Leadership

* Serve as the organization's primary strategic leader and public ambassador.
* Lead implementation of the strategic plan while identifying opportunities for future growth and innovation.
* Foster a collaborative, mission\-focused culture centered on educational excellence, belonging, accountability, and student success.
* Build strategic partnerships that increase organizational visibility, strengthen community engagement, and expand opportunities for scholars.
* Continuously evaluate organizational effectiveness and drive continuous improvement.

Operations \& Financial Management

* Oversee all organizational operations, administrative systems, and day\-to\-day management.
* Partner with the Treasurer and Finance Committee to develop and monitor annual budgets and financial forecasts.
* Ensure sound fiscal stewardship, financial transparency, and compliance with nonprofit regulations and grant requirements.
* Establish efficient operational systems, organizational policies, and performance metrics.
* Maintain appropriate risk management and internal controls.

People Leadership

* Recruit, supervise, coach, and develop staff, resident staff, and volunteers.
* Create a culture of collaboration, accountability, professional growth, and high performance.
* Strengthen volunteer engagement and create meaningful leadership opportunities for community members.
* Ensure personnel practices support organizational excellence and mission achievement.

Community Relations \& Communications

* Serve as the primary spokesperson and representative of Ridgefield ABC.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with students, families, alumni, volunteers, schools, donors, civic leaders, and community partners.
* Partner with the Communications Committee to elevate the organization's visibility through storytelling, media relations, digital communications, and community outreach.
* Represent Ridgefield ABC at community events, speaking engagements, and partnership meetings.

Student\-Centered Leadership

* Foster a supportive, inclusive environment where scholars are empowered to succeed academically and personally.
* Respond to sensitive situations with professionalism, empathy, sound judgment, and integrity.
* Collaborate with resident staff and program leaders to ensure student well\-being remains at the center of organizational decision\-making.
* Promote a culture grounded in safety, belonging, leadership, and educational opportunity.

Board Partnership \& Governance

The Executive Director serves as the Board of Directors' primary strategic partner and works closely with Board leadership to advance the organization's mission and governance responsibilities.

Responsibilities include:

* Supporting implementation of the Board's strategic priorities.
* Providing regular reports on organizational performance, finances, fundraising, and program outcomes.
* Partnering with Board committees on fundraising, governance, strategic planning, and organizational sustainability.
* Preparing materials for Board and committee meetings and ensuring effective communication between the Board and staff.

jobs.title: Nonprofit Leadership, Fundraising, Education (Ridgefield ABC) - Executive Director
jobs.description:
Executive Director \| Nonprofit Leadership, Fundraising, Educational Equity \& Community ImpactRidgefield A Better Chance (RABC)

Ridgefield A Better Chance (RABC) is part of the national A Better Chance network, one of the nation’s leading educational equity organizations dedicated to increasing access to high\-quality college preparatory education for academically talented students of color.

Since 1987, Ridgefield ABC has empowered young women of color through educational opportunity, leadership development, academic enrichment, and community support. Scholars live in a residential setting supported by residential directors, academic advisors, host families, college counseling, and dedicated volunteers while attending the academically rigorous Ridgefield High School.

Ridgefield ABC is a respected and mission\-driven nonprofit organization with a highly engaged Board of Directors and strong community support. We are seeking a dynamic, relationship\-centered Executive Director to lead fundraising, nonprofit operations, strategic growth, community engagement, student\-centered programming, and organizational leadership.

The Executive Director reports directly to the Board President and works collaboratively with the Board of Directors, volunteers, staff, families, community stakeholders, and national ABC partners.

Position Overview

The Executive Director serves as the strategic and operational leader of Ridgefield ABC and is responsible for advancing the organization’s mission through fundraising leadership, donor engagement, nonprofit management, financial oversight, community partnerships, organizational growth, and program support.

The ideal candidate is a collaborative and adaptable nonprofit leader with demonstrated experience in fundraising, relationship\-building, nonprofit operations, volunteer engagement, and team leadership who is passionate about educational equity, youth development, diversity, inclusion, and student success.

Key Leadership ResponsibilitiesFundraising, Development \& Donor Engagement

* Minimum 4–6 years of demonstrated nonprofit fundraising and development experience with success securing revenue through donor cultivation, sponsorships, grants, fundraising campaigns, and community partnerships
* Develop and execute comprehensive fundraising and donor engagement strategies to support organizational sustainability and growth
* Cultivate, solicit, steward, and retain individual donors, corporate sponsors, business partners, and community stakeholders
* Partner with the Fundraising Committee to oversee annual fundraising campaigns, donor events, sponsorship opportunities, and community engagement initiatives
* Identify grant and foundation opportunities and collaborate with grant writing support
* Strengthen donor pipelines, donor retention, and long\-term philanthropic relationships
* Support fundraising strategy aligned with educational equity, leadership development, and student success goals

Strategic Leadership \& Organizational Growth

* Serve as the strategic leader and primary ambassador of the organization
* Advance organizational priorities aligned with educational equity, student achievement, and leadership development
* Support long\-term sustainability, strategic planning, organizational growth, and operational excellence
* Foster a mission\-driven, collaborative, inclusive, and student\-centered organizational culture
* Develop partnerships that increase visibility, community support, and organizational impact

Operations, Financial Oversight \& Administration

* Oversee nonprofit operations, financial management, organizational systems, and administrative processes
* Partner with the Treasurer and Board leadership on annual budgeting, forecasting, and financial oversight
* Ensure compliance with nonprofit regulations, grant requirements, reporting standards, and organizational policies
* Maintain effective operational systems, program tracking tools, and organizational records
* Monitor operational effectiveness and organizational performance metrics

Talent Recruitment, Volunteer Engagement \& Team Leadership

* Recruit, supervise, support, and develop staff and volunteers
* Oversee resident staff and personnel\-related processes
* Foster strong communication, collaboration, accountability, and team engagement
* Build meaningful volunteer opportunities and stakeholder involvement
* Support leadership development and professional growth within the organization

Community Engagement, Marketing \& Representation

* Serve as a primary representative and spokesperson for Ridgefield ABC within the community
* Build strong relationships with schools, families, volunteers, alumni, donors, and community partners
* Partner with the Communications Committee to increase awareness through public relations, storytelling, social media, outreach campaigns, and community engagement
* Represent the organization at community events, meetings, and partnership opportunities

Crisis Management \& Student\-Centered Leadership

* Respond to sensitive or high\-impact situations with professionalism, empathy, sound judgment, and calm leadership
* Apply thoughtful problem\-solving and resource coordination when addressing organizational or student\-related challenges
* Support a student\-centered environment grounded in equity, care, leadership, accountability, and belonging

Board Collaboration \& Governance

The Executive Director works closely with the Board of Directors and Committee Chairs to support organizational leadership, governance, and strategic planning.

Responsibilities include:

* Providing regular organizational, operational, and programmatic updates to the Board and Executive Committee
* Collaborating on fundraising, sustainability, and organizational growth initiatives

jobs.title: Director of Communications
jobs.description:
**About Us**
------------


Anthos\|Home is a nonprofit startup focused on creating a new way home for New Yorkers with housing vouchers. We streamline the apartment search, approval, and move\-in process using technology to move people into stable housing faster. We believe every person deserves a place to blossom, and we're building the tools, systems, and teams to make that possible.


**Our Culture**
---------------


At Anthos\|Home, urgency drives our work because every day in shelter matters. We are focused on helping people move into stable housing as quickly as possible and continuously improving how we get there.


We are looking for team members who are highly data\-oriented, tech\-savvy, and comfortable learning new systems. Strong candidates value accuracy, use data to inform decisions, and actively improve how information is tracked and shared.


We value people who are thoughtful, adaptable, and detail\-oriented, and who follow through, communicate openly, and take ownership of their work. How effectively we use data and technology directly impacts how quickly people get home.


**The Role**
------------


The Director of Communications will report directly to the CEO and develop, maintain, and execute AnthoslHome's organization\-wide communications strategy and manage all internal and external communications at a critical moment in the organization's growth. This role presents the rare opportunity to shape the public voice of one of New York City's most innovative housing nonprofits \- defining how we tell the story of our organization and the system we're working to change.


The Director of Communications builds and stewards the organization's brand, leads day\-to\-day content creation and engagement across channels, and ensures consistent voice and messaging across every audience the organization reaches. Reporting directly to the CEO, the Director of Communications works in close collaboration with the Vice President of Development and Chief of Staff. The Director of Communications will also receive support from the Executive Associate.


The ideal candidate is a strategic, hands\-on communicator who pairs strong editorial judgment with deep storytelling instincts and a track record of translating programmatic and policy work into compelling content. They thrive in a fast\-paced startup environment, take ownership across both strategy and execution, and are energized by the chance to build the communications infrastructure that will elevate the essential work of AnthoslHome.

  

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------


**Communications Strategy \& Brand**


* Develop, maintain, and execute AnthoslHome's organization\-wide communications strategy in alignment with the organization's mission, programmatic priorities, and growth goals
* Oversee the development of a unified communication framework and execution of strategies for communicating with participants through each phase of their interaction with AnthoslHome.
* Build and maintain core communications infrastructure, including the organization's communications calendar and internal approval pathways
* Maintain and evolve AnthoslHome's organizational style guide, ensuring brand and voice consistency across all internal and external materials
* Oversee AnthoslHome's website, including content updates, ongoing maintenance, and strategic positioning
* Manage engagement with communications consultants to ensure alignment with organizational messaging and strategic priorities

  

**External Communications**


* Lead day\-to\-day content creation, publication, and engagement across all external channels, including social media, e\-newsletters, and other digital communications
* Identify opportunities across the organization to conduct interviews, capture stories, and create content that supports core messaging and showcases organizational impact
* Identify and pursue opportunities for speaking engagements, publicity, and media that are in line with the organization's communication strategy
* Build and maintain relationships with media, partner organizations, and other external stakeholders relevant to brand and visibility
* Develop communications materials that celebrate accomplishments and increase organizational visibility across diverse stakeholder audiences
* Collaborate with the Development team on the refinement of communications to donor audiences
* Provide editorial review and brand alignment for communication materials produced across the organization, and with our external partners such as landlords as well as participants.

  

**Internal and Executive Communications**


* Ensure consistent brand and voice across internally produced materials, including operational, training, and onboarding content
* Draft and manage internal and external communications on behalf of the CEO related to strategy, organizational priorities, and stakeholder relations

  

**Cross\-Functional Collaboration**

  

* Collaborate with Program, Development, and Operations teams to surface stories, align messaging, and represent communications priorities in organizational planning
* Coordinate with the Chief of Staff and Chief Program Officer on policy and advocacy messaging
* Support Program teams in developing and refining marketing materials for target audiences, including property providers
* Prepare staff and participants on messaging content and delivery for speaking engagements, events, and other communications opportunities
* Support the Development team on the communications components of fundraising campaigns, donor stewardship materials, and event needs
* Carry out other related duties as assigned in support of organizational goals and team operations


**Qualifications**
------------------


* Bachelor's degree required
* 10\+ years of progressive experience in communications, marketing, or a related field, ideally in a nonprofit or mission\-driven environment
* Demonstrated experience developing and executing org

jobs.title: Chief Development Officer
jobs.description:
**Chief Development Officer –****Immediate Full Time Opportunity**


**Bronx, NY**



RiverSpring Living is seeking a Director of Sales to join our dynamic team!



The RiverSpring Living campus is set on 32 acres overlooking the Hudson River, in Riverdale, New York. RiverSpring Living provides a full range of care solutions including nursing home care, managed long term care, assisted living programs, senior housing, rehabilitation services and specialized services such as elder abuse prevention and memory care.


**Position Overview**



The Chief Development Officer will provide overall fund\-raising leadership and implementation including planning, management and direction of the development initiatives. The position is also responsible for all fundraising efforts including creation and implementation of capital and annual campaigns, major gifts, deferred gifts, foundation, corporate and government support, direct/electronic mail and events as well as research.


**Primary Responsibilities:**


* Directs the day to day operation of Development Department
* Build and maintain relationships with volunteers, prospects, donors, government leaders, business leaders and other prospective donors
* Work with and support the board of trustees in all facets of solicitation and stewardship
* Responsibe for management of the development staff and consultants in all development activities and working with other departments to develop funding strategies for specific programs


**Qualifications:**


* Bachelor’s degree, Masters preferred, with 7\+ years of progressive management and fundraising experience
* Must have a proven professional record of success, be able to multitask and display a professional manner to be able to interact effectively with administrators, staff, volunteers, donors and potential donors
* Excellent written and oral communication skills
* Knowledge of Microsoft Office and Raisers Edge Required


**Disclaimer**



This job description is not intended to be an exhaustive list of duties, responsibilities, or qualifications. Management reserves the right to modify or assign other duties as necessary.


**Compensation:**


* Annual Salary: $275,000 \- $300,000
* Benefits include health insurance, retirement plans, paid time off, and professional development opportunities.

  


**Equal Opportunity Employer (EOE)**

jobs.title: Managing Director, Gift Planning (Legacy Giving)
jobs.description:
ABOUT BCRF



Breast cancer is a complex disease with no simple solution. Research is the key to stopping it in its tracks. Founded in 1993 by Evelyn H. Lauder, the Breast Cancer Research Foundation is the largest private funder of breast cancer research in the world. By investing in the best minds in science to examine every aspect of the disease from prevention to metastasis—and fostering cross\-disciplinary collaboration—BCRF is accelerating the entire field and moves us closer to the answers we urgently need to be the end of breast cancer.



About the Role



The Breast Cancer Research Foundation seeks a strategic, entrepreneurial, and experienced Managing Director, Gift Planning (Legacy Giving) to lead and grow the Foundation's legacy giving program.



Reporting to the Chief Philanthropy Officer, the Managing Director will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive planned giving strategy that inspires transformational philanthropy through bequests, beneficiary designations, blended gifts, and other charitable planning vehicles. The Managing Director will help donors align their philanthropic goals with BCRF's mission while growing a robust pipeline of future support. The Managing Director will manage a portfolio of donors and prospects, oversee marketing and stewardship initiatives, partner closely colleagues across the Development \& Events team, and help shape the long\-term vision and infrastructure for a growing program.



This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced planned giving professional who enjoys both frontline fundraising and program building. The successful candidate will bring deep expertise in charitable gift planning, strong relationship\-building skills, and a demonstrated ability to create systems and strategies that support long\-term growth.



As the program grows, this role will play an important role in shaping its future structure and staffing. The Managing Director will oversee significant vendor partnerships, including FreeWill and Stelter, manage the program’s marketing budget, and lead a team of two full\-time employees. The program also benefits from the partnership of experienced pro bono legal counsel, who advise on complex and/or significant gift intentions and realized bequests. The program receives outstanding support from pro\-bono legal counsel, who provide guidance on complex and/or significant gift intentions and actualized.



This position is based in New York City and currently requires staff to work onsite a minimum of three days per week.



Primary Responsibilities



Strategic Leadership and Program Growth


* Develop and implement a multi\-year strategic plan to grow BCRF's planned giving program with a focus on increasing legacy commitments and stewardship of existing donors.
* Manage and achieve annual goals and performance metrics, regularly evaluating program effectiveness and identifying opportunities for growth.
* Identify opportunities to integrate planned giving across the donor lifecycle, including Annual Giving, Major Gifts, Principal Gifts, and Events.
* Partner with Major Gifts colleagues to incorporate gift planning strategies into donor conversations and fundraising plans.
* Maintain expertise in planned giving trends, charitable tax law developments, and best practices in philanthropy and estate planning.
* Serve as BCRF's internal subject matter expert on charitable gift planning and wealth transfer philanthropy.
* Represent BCRF externally with donors, advisors, professional organizations, and industry partners.


Donor Cultivation, Solicitation, and Stewardship


* Manage a portfolio of planned giving donors and prospects and develop individualized cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship strategies.
* Develop and implement gift planning strategies tailored to donors' philanthropic, financial, and estate planning objectives.
* Personally identify, qualify, cultivate, solicit, and secure planned gifts.
* Build trusted relationships with donors, family members, attorneys, financial advisors, trustees, executors, and other professional advisors.
* Collaborate with Major Gifts colleagues to identify and pursue blended gift opportunities that maximize donor impact.
* Prepare proposals, stewardship reports, and other donor\-facing materials.
* Develop strategies to increase legacy society membership and deepen engagement among existing members of the Evelyn H. Lauder Legacy Society.


Marketing and Communications


* Lead the strategic direction of BCRF's planned giving marketing program across print, digital, direct response, and donor engagement channels.
* Oversee relationships with planned giving vendors, consultants, and external partners.
* Partner with Marketing \& Communications to maintain compelling and technically accurate planned giving content and educational materials.
* Monitor campaign performance and use data to refine donor acquisition, retention, and conversion strategies.


Estate Administration and Program Operations


* Provide strategic oversight of bequest administration in partnership with external legal counsel.
* Ensure timely and accurate management of planned gift records, estate documentation, donor agreements, and reporting.
* Partner closely with the Database \& Donor Services team to maintain data integrity and reporting accuracy.
* Develop systems, workflows, and policies that support operational excellence and scalability.
* Monitor and analyze program performance, pipeline development, and realized revenue to identify opportunities for growth.


Team Leadership


* Recruit, supervise, mentor, and develop planned giving staff as the program grows.
* Foster a collaborative and donor\-centered culture that emphasizes accountability, professionalism, and excellence.
* Provide planned giving expertise and training to colleagues across the Development \& Events team.


About You



You are a strategic, relationship\-driven fundraising professional with deep gift pla

jobs.title: Elementary Program Director
jobs.description:
The Police Athletic League (PAL) of New York City is an independent youth development nonprofit organization and has been a vibrant and vital institution, serving as an effective, critical bridge between police and communities across all five boroughs of the City since 1914\. PAL operates diverse programs for children as young as 2 years old to young adults. Programs include Head Start, after\-school programs and summer day camps for elementary, middle and high school youth, and summer Playstreets. PAL also runs evening teen centers and college readiness programs, youth employment, and juvenile justice programs. Staying true to PAL’s roots, a cornerstone of our youth development strategy includes offering competitive and recreational sports for children of all ages. At PAL, we’re devoted to empowering communities and building the best places to work, and that goes for our own teams too.


In its 110th year, PAL continues to reimagine the agency’s leadership structure to ensure the next 110 years are nimble, strategic, responsive, and efficient. We are looking for COMPASS School Program Directors to elevate our out\-of\-school programming embedded within a New York City public school or PAL Center. Locations may serve Elementary through High School youth through SEL, STEM, Literacy, Sports, Arts, Mental Health, and College and Career Access Programming.


This position reports to the Program Supervisor.


Elementary Afterschool Program Directors are key on\-site supervisors managing all programming as well as relationships with youth, parents, school community, and the community at large. Directors must be creative programmers, skilled supervisors, and skilled talent developers. Familiarity with SACC regulations and DYCD funding requirements is a plus. Directors will partner with the NYPD, DOH, DYCD, NYCPS, and local community organizations. We are looking for individuals who can listen, problem solve and build bridges to partnerships that expand opportunities for NYC’s youth. As such:


* Candidates must be dedicated to learning and implementing Best Practices in the field of youth development.
* Candidates must be able to support programming that happens during the day, evenings, and weekends.
* Candidates must support the ideas and opportunities of the youth, staff, and community.
* Candidates must possess excellent communication and listening skills.
* Candidates must work together as a team to support PAL’s programming throughout the entire City.

**Major Duties \& Responsibilities**

* Foster social and emotional competencies and mental and physical well\-being in a safe, welcoming, inclusive, and nurturing environment.
* Manage all aspects of PAL Elementary School Afterschool Programming including overseeing the implementation of mental health support and inclusion coordination, identifying resources to support youth with special needs. Use identified resources to support youth with special needs and/or mental health needs, complete mental health referrals, and/or activate DYCD’s inclusions resources.
* Recruit, hire, and provide excellent supervision and support to all part time Group Leaders and Activity Specialists, Administrative Assistants, and Content Specialists.
* Ensure the program is aligned to strategic objectives of PAL, policies and procedures, agency programming calendar, SACC regulations, and the contractual obligations of government and private funding sources including fire safety certification.
* Develops a strong Youth Development culture within the Center ensuring voice, growth, and advancement for all youth and staff. Train, conduct observations, and coach across all programs ensuring SEL, mental health, and inclusion policies are being implemented as designed.
* Coordinates with the Principal, Director, Regional Director, SYEP Director, and Head Quarters staff to grow creative programming opportunities unique to the Site.
* Collects program data, analyzes and drives PAL forward with data driven recommendations.
* Ensure strong reciprocal partnerships with NYPD, NYCPS school partner, DYCD, DOH and other Community partners aligned with PAL’s mission.
* Plans and coordinates various citywide programs, activities, and special events.
* Assists in the development of future funding grants and requests for proposals.
* Provides mediation and troubleshooting for on\-site issues.
* Respond appropriately when time\-sensitive mental health services are needed such as suicide prevention, grief, and loss due to death or incarceration of a family member.

**What You Bring:**


* An associate's in child development, education, physical education or recreation or related and two years of direct experience working with youth under 13 OR a NYS Children’s Program Administrator Credential and 2 years of experience working with youth under 13 OR a SACC Credential and 2 years of experience working with youth under 13 OR two years in college with 18 credits in child development, education, physical education or recreation or related and two years of direct experience working with youth under 13\.
* A minimum of 4 years of demonstrated successful experience in a supervisory position providing services to youth that includes program management and staff supervision with at least three years in a leadership capacity.
* Demonstrated skills in program planning, implementation, and evaluation.
* Open to learning and growing. All staff will be learning mental health, SEL, and special needs inclusion skills and expected to integrate them into programming.
* Knowledge of DYCD Connect preferred. Microsoft office skills are required.
* Strong verbal and written communication skills and ability to work collaboratively. Bi\-lingual in Spanish, French, or Chinese preferred.
* Able to travel to any PAL location in the 5 Boroughs for training.
* Work Schedule: Monday to Friday, 10:00\-6:00pm with weekends and nights as needed.

**Compensation:** The salary for this role is $68,000\-70,000 annually.
